text: Cal Newport's concept of "digital minimalism" is not a rejection of technology but a philosophy of deliberate adoption. Where most people accumulate digital tools passively — downloading apps because they are free, keeping social media because others use it — Newport argues for a different standard: a tool earns its place only if it substantially supports something you deeply value, and no less-costly alternative exists. This inversion of the default question, from "why not?" to "why?", forces users to articulate what they actually want from their attention before committing it to a platform.
question: According to the passage, what standard must a digital tool meet under Newport's philosophy to justify its use?

options.text: It must be used by a significant portion of the user's social network
It must be free or low-cost compared to alternatives
It must substantially support a deeply held value with no less-costly alternative
It must demonstrably improve productivity over a 30-day trial period
